title: draba lactea var. pubescens
intro: Draba lactea var. pubescens, also known as the hairy white draba, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and hairy leaves. The medicinal properties of D. lactea var. pubescens are attributed to its high concentration of glucosinolates, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of D. lactea var. pubescens can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and support the body's natural healing processes.
